Issue: Fix compute_shape_offset non-tuple indexing for PyTorch >=2.9
Decision: Cast spatial_shape to a tuple inside compute_shape_offset to prevent breaking changes in PyTorch >= 2.9
Rationale: Prevent breaking changes in PyTorch >= 2.9 by ensuring spatial_shape is a tuple before passing to np.array in compute_shape_offset
Author: Harrisaint
Cost: Low (simple fix with minimal resource impact)
Risk: Low (minimal risk as it's a simple type conversion to prevent compatibility issues)

Argument.author: coderabbitai[bot]
Argument.argument: PyTorch >=2.9 introduces non-tuple sequence indexing which breaks existing code using compute_shape_offset with non-tuple spatial_shape inputs. The fix ensures compatibility by casting spatial_shape to a tuple before processing.

Argument.author: walkthrough
Argument.argument: Converting spatial_shape to a tuple before passing it to np.array(...) in monai/data/utils.py avoids PyTorch ≥2.9 non-tuple sequence indexing issues. A new unit test is added to verify the fix with torch.Size([10, 10, 10]) input.
